title: Platform Content (everything outside the 18 income modules)
description: Curriculum, assessment design, workflow, languages, supplementary modules, and programme structure for the mobile-first, low-bandwidth AI builder app.

overview.summary: This application is tailored for mobile users in low-bandwidth environments. The curriculum prioritises local-first strategies, core theoretical concepts, token efficiency, and mobile-friendly offline single-file tools.
overview.philosophy: Participants are treated not merely as ‘users’ of AI but as builders of local digital assets: datasets, reports, scripts, translations, templates, research summaries, service menus, and community knowledge products.

curriculum.description: Four core theory modules, each led by an expert persona. Every module contains eight sequential sub-modules.
curriculum.structure_rule: Each module has eight sections; the eight sections present the sub-modules in sequential format.

modules.title: AI Fundamentals
modules.focus: Focuses on the core paradigms, scaling realities, and hardware constraints that dictate how AI functions under the hood.

sub_modules.title: Core Concepts & the Spectrum of AI
sub_modules.description: Distinguishing between Artificial Intelligence (AI), Generative AI, and the theoretical baseline of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).

sub_modules.title: The Architecture Shift
sub_modules.description: Understanding the transition from early rule-based symbolic systems (“AI winters”) to modern probabilistic and statistical machine learning models.

sub_modules.title: Transformers & GPT Archetypes
sub_modules.description: Demystifying the decoder-only transformer architecture, parallel sequence processing, and token-by-token text generation.

sub_modules.title: Hardware Scaling & Compute
sub_modules.description: How parallel processing works via massive GPU core clusters and application-specific integrated circuits like Google’s Tensor Processing Units (TPUs).

sub_modules.title: Multi-Dimensional Tensors
sub_modules.description: Visualizing data structures from 0D scalars and 1D vectors to n-dimensional tensors that handle modern AI workloads.

sub_modules.title: Kaplan vs. Chinchilla Scaling Laws
sub_modules.description: Balancing parameters, dataset sizes, and compute-optimal training requirements (~20 tokens per parameter).

sub_modules.title: Mosaic’s Law & Economics
sub_modules.description: Navigating the rapid deflation of computing costs (1/4 cost per year for static capabilities) to keep local setups competitive.

sub_modules.title: LLMs vs. Small Language Models (SLMs)
sub_modules.description: Evaluating resource footprints to choose highly efficient, domain-specific models for edge/mobile devices.

modules.title: Corpus Stewardship
modules.focus: Focuses on linguistic preservation, structuring data repositories, mitigating bias, and commercializing authentic local speech artifacts.

sub_modules.title: Knowledge Representation & Logic
sub_modules.description: Mapping real-world cultural speech nuances into deterministic frameworks and machine-usable formats.

sub_modules.title: Ontologies for Authentic Speech
sub_modules.description: Building formal specifications of categories, definitions, and linguistic relationships within a specific language or community.

sub_modules.title: The Symbol-Grounding Problem
sub_modules.description: Overcoming the barrier where models process tokens mechanically without understanding physical or cultural reality.

sub_modules.title: Unsupervised Pre-training Patterns
sub_modules.description: Understanding how models ingest raw text tokens to discover syntax, grammar, and systemic vocabulary habits.

sub_modules.title: SFT & RLHF Alignment
sub_modules.description: How Supervised Fine-Tuning and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback refine raw predictors into safe, conversational assets.

sub_modules.title: Mitigating Cognitive & Cultural Bias
sub_modules.description: Practical methods to decouple subjective descriptors and instruct models to evaluate multi-perspective data.

sub_modules.title: Echo Chambers vs. Hallucinations
sub_modules.description: Identifying statistical failures where models confidently invent data or loops that narrow output diversity.

sub_modules.title: IP, Attribution, & Data Sovereignty
sub_modules.description: Navigating the ethics of training data, respecting original creators, and commercializing community speech assets safely.

modules.title: Prompt Efficiency
modules.focus: Focuses on maximizing contextual output quality while minimising data transmission and operational tokens.

sub_modules.title: Shot-Prompting Methodologies
sub_modules.description: Mastering zero-shot instruction, one-shot patterns, and few-shot structuring to maximize accuracy on the first try.

sub_modules.title: Self-Consistency & Meta-Prompting
sub_modules.description: Instructing models to evaluate alternative reasoning paths and optimize their own programmatic inputs.

sub_modules.title: The RTF Core Framework
sub_modules.description: Structuring concise text inputs explicitly around targeted Roles, Tasks, and Formats.

sub_modules.title: Deep Dive into CO-STAR
sub_modules.description: Standardizing inputs across Context, Objective, Style, Tone, Audience, and Response formats to prevent vague outputs.

sub_modules.title: Hybrid Frameworks (ERA, RISE, CREATE)
sub_modules.description: Selecting specialized templates for rapid code delegation, multi-step tasks, or business evaluations.

sub_modules.title: Troubleshooting Prompts (The RSTI Protocol)
sub_modules.description: Debugging and separating broken or circular logic when a model fails to follow parameters.

sub_modules.title: Text-Only Cache-Offline Patterns
sub_modules.description: Engineering single-exchange prompts to deliver an entire workflow layout in one compressed response.

sub_modules.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) vs. Agents
sub_modules.description: Distinguishing between searching flat vector databases and running dynamic, loop-driven systems.

modules.title: Microenterprise
modules.focus: Focuses on local data analysis, offline infrastructure deployment, multi-agent logic, and automated client delivery.

sub_modules.title: Antifragile Operations Strategy
sub_modules.description: Building systems that improve through volatility, internet scarcity, and physical infrastructure constraints.

sub_modules.title: Local Data Manipulation Stack
sub_modules.description: Managing tabular information, relational queries, schemas, and dynamic arrays completely offline.

sub_modules.title: Local Lookup Engineering
sub_modules.description: Mastering exact-match lookups, array positioning, and cross-source transformations via tools like Power Query.

sub_modules.title: Cross-Platform Capability Snapshots
sub_modules.description: Choosing the right foundational model family based on their specialised technical or coding strengths.

sub_modules.title: Local Model Orchestration (Msty Track)
sub_modules.description: Running open-source models completely offline on your own device hardware.

sub_modules.title: Multi-Agent Logic & Crews
sub_modules.description: Designing personas, discrete tasks, and modular operational pipelines using hierarchical Python structures.

sub_modules.title: Secure Tool Handovers
sub_modules.description: Decoupling credentials, packaging sensitive values into .env templates, and exporting blueprints as JSON.

sub_modules.title: Network Resilience Design
sub_modules.description: Structuring automated workflows that save intermediate logs to resume execution instead of suffering expensive cloud restarts.

system_integration.title: System Integration for Testing & Mobile Coding

interactive_agent_assessment_loop.description: To test users, the app’s agents execute a text-driven evaluation cycle mapped directly to the active sub-module.

phases.name: Question Phase
phases.description: The agent serves a single contextual concept or scenario question.

phases.name: User Input Evaluation
phases.description: The user provides an open-ended text response.

phases.name: Feedback & Scoring
phases.description: The agent scores the accuracy, fixes core misunderstandings, and prompts the user for the next task without using bandwidth-heavy visual components.

mobile_offline_coding_framework.description: Because users write code directly on their phones in low-bandwidth regions, the agents explicitly train them to output single-file HTML/CSS/JavaScript environments.
mobile_offline_coding_framework.boilerplate:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Offline Microenterprise Template</title>
    <style>/* Mobile-first CSS styling */</style>
</head>
<body>
    <script>
        // Pure JavaScript Logic with localStorage persistence
    </script>
</body>
</html>

principles.name: Zero-Server Footprint
principles.description: Code requires no external build tools, servers, or packages.

principles.name: Browser-Native Execution
principles.description: Users execute their projects locally by saving them on their device storage and opening them through standard mobile web browsers.

principles.name: Persistent Storage
principles.description: Data is retained natively on the mobile device via the browser’s local storage capabilities.

module_bank_intro.summary: A choose-your-own module bank for a mobile-first AI income programme. The design assumes unreliable electricity, low bandwidth, and intermittent access to paid AI tools.
module_bank_intro.rationale: The model is timely: the World Bank describes ‘Small AI’ as practical AI that can run on everyday devices such as mobile phones, naming connectivity, compute, context/data, and competency/skills as foundations for inclusive AI ecosystems. The economic need is clear: UNCTAD notes that least developed countries remain weakly represented in digitally deliverable service exports, while the Global Findex 2025 reports that 84% of adults in low- and middle-income countries own a mobile phone and 3 billion have smartphones.

shared_energy_to_income_workflow.title: Shared Energy-to-Income Workflow
shared_energy_to_income_workflow.note: Mobile money is an especially relevant payment pathway in many African contexts because financial inclusion in Sub-Saharan Africa has grown substantially through mobile money account adoption.

languages.note: The app teaches users how to build each asset step-by-step in English; students can turn on translator mode using valid and authentic translation apps.

supplementary_modules.description: Extra modules teaching practical mobile/office skills.
supplementary_modules.structure_rule: Each module has eight sections; the eight sections present the sub-sections in sequential format.
topics: Excel: creating spreadsheets and entering formulas
Compressing files on phones / mobile devices

programme_structure.title: Entrepreneurship Programme Structure
programme_structure.access_note: A separate section of the app that can be accessed at any time.

weeks.title: Phone as Production Studio
weeks.description: Battery discipline, offline folders, file naming, compression, CSV basics, mobile money safety, scam awareness.

weeks.title: AI Basics
weeks.description: Prompting, hallucinations, verification, multilingual output, responsible AI.

weeks.title: Choose One Income Module
weeks.description: Each participant selects one income module and creates a first sellable sample.

weeks.title: Community Client Pilot
weeks.description: Participants serve one real local client: vendor, student, cooperative, school, clinic, or NGO.

weeks.title: Packaging and Pricing
weeks.description: Each participant creates a one-page service menu in French, Brazilian Portuguese, Spanish, English, and Wolof.

weeks.title: Cooperative Marketplace
weeks.description: Participants form teams: data team, language team, marketing team, research team, business-support team.

core_rule: The goal is not merely to teach people to ‘use AI.’ The goal is to help them create ownable, reusable, locally valuable digital assets from the knowledge, languages, markets, and social realities that already exist in their communities.

references.label: World Bank
references.title: Digital Progress and Trends Report 2025 — AI foundations

references.label: World Bank
references.title: Financial inclusion in Sub-Saharan Africa overview

references.label: UNESCO Courier
references.title: African languages: a blind spot in AI

conversion_notes: The 18 income modules are delivered in the companion file income_modules.json.
Modules 15 and 18 carried an asterisk in the source and are expanded re-statements of modules 1 and 2 respectively; they are flagged with `expanded_variant_of` in income_modules.json.
Source inconsistency: Programme Week 3 references ‘one of the 11 modules,’ while the income bank contains 18 entries. Text preserved verbatim; reconcile the count when wiring the app.

The source contained stray editorial/meta-commentary lines (e.g. a remark about ‘seven substantive modules’ and a missing ‘Module 8’); these were generation artifacts and were not imported as content.
URL tracking parameters (e.g. ?utm_source=chatgpt.com) were stripped from citations for cleanliness.
